metadata.title: SYNGO_BP_3631
metadata.comment:
Rationale: 14/5/2018 Pim
rule: NAS_CPLX_2
rationale: Polyribosomes are present in dendritic spines (PMID:12165474, electron microscopy). The ribosome is the central holoenzyme in the process of "translation (GO:0006412)". RL40 is a generally accepted constituent of the ribosome that together with other ribosomal subunits contributes to the intrinsic activity of the ribosomal complex.. No functions of RL40 are known independent from the ribosomal complex. Inference to term "translation (GO:0006412)" occurs_in "postsynapse (GO:0098794:)" is trivial.
C_ref CC: PMID:12165474 (dendritic spine head)
P_inf CC: SynGO ID #608
P_inf CC additional:
- Biesemann et al.(2014), PMID:24413018, TableS1, prep:synapse
- Takamori et al.(2006), PMID:17110340, TableS1, prep:sv
- Distler et al.(2014), PMID:25211037, Table S1, prep:synapse
- Fernandez et al.(2009), PMID:19455133, TableS1, prep:psd
Complex membership: RL40 protein is a fixed member of the 60S ribosomal subunit complex.
- Shown in crystal structure (PMID:28138070, see Fig.1, name: el40)
- GO annotations to "structural constituent of ribosome (GO:0003735)" (ISO and IEA). Experimental description: Briefly adult mouse brains (2-16 months, 20 animals, 10 g wet weight) were homogenized ... p. 915
